text: Robotic low anterior resection for a distal sigmoid colon cancer. We have the following disclosures here. A fifty-two year old male presents with three months of abdominal pain and hematochesia. Patient has no family history of GI cancer. A physical exam revealed internal hemorrhoids and a BMI of twenty-eight. Colonoscopy showed a friable circumferential mass twenty centimeters from the anal verge. The colonoscope could not be advanced proximal to the mass. Biopsies were then taken and the mass was tattooed. Biopsy results showed that there was a microscopic focus of high-grade dysplasia or intramucosal adenocarcinoma. A CT chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed, which were negative for distant metastases. MRI of the abdomen and pelvis showed a seven-centimeter segment of distal sigmoid colon, fifteen centimeters above the anal verge with diffuse thickening of the submucosal and muscularis margins.

Key points include team personal protective precautions, an N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N-N

Before making the extraction incision, the insufflator is turned off and the pneumoperitoneum is emptied via the smoke evacuation device. Trans-anal specimen extraction is also avoided to prevent release of pneumoperitoneum. At our institution, we use a Buffalo filter plume port active, which allows for pneumoperitoneum and smoke evacuation through ultrafiltration. A full control dial on the plume port active allows us to control the rate of suction. This whole system is connected to regular suction in the room. Advantages of robotic surgery during the COVID era include lower levels of personal protective equipment needed for the console surgeon. Visualization is also adequate with reduced intra-abdominal pressures to eight millimeters of mercury. There is also an ability to minimize electrocautery power by avoiding the use of ultrasonic sealing devices and by easily using clips and sutures.

The surgical team utilizes four ports, including a 12mm port in the right lower quadrant and three 8.5mm ports. The robotic arms are positioned towards the left lower quadrant. The sigmoid colon is visualized in the pelvis, and interloop adhesions proximal to the tumor are sharply lysed. Adhesions between the mesentery and sigmoid colon are also lysed. The tumor is adherent to the lateral pelvic wall. A medial-to-lateral approach is employed, using a cardiac retractor for anterior traction of the sigmoid colon to incise the peritoneum and create an aortic mesenteric window. The hypogastric nerves and left ureter are identified and preserved. A sponge aids in retracting the small bowel to visualize the origin of the inferior mesenteric artery, which is skeletonized and ligated with hemoclips.

Following the ligation of the inferior mesenteric artery, the surgeon continues the dissection of the mesocolon using a medial-to-lateral approach, carefully avoiding injury to the inferior mesenteric vein and ureter. The inferior mesenteric vein is skeletonized, ligated, and divided with hemoclips just below the pancreas. The omental bursa is entered, and dissection proceeds to the splenic flexure, with the left colon retracted medially to divide lateral attachments up to the splenic flexure.

The surgeon performs a partial mesorectal resection, mobilizing the rectum posteriorly by sharp dissection between the mesorectal fascia and presacral fascia. The right side is mobilized, followed by the left mesorectum, with circumferential dissection connected anteriorly. The fourth arm retracts the sigmoid colon to maintain the correct plane, ensuring adequate distal margin from the tumor. Dissection proceeds posteriorly, then laterally on the right and left, and finally anteriorly.

The surgeon divides the rectum distal to the tumor using multiple 45mm green loads. After undocking the robot, the robotic arms are repositioned to continue mobilizing the splenic flexure. The gastrocolic ligament is sequentially divided from right to left using a vessel sealer. The mobilized descending and sigmoid colon is checked for adequate mobilization to the level of the peritoneal reflection.

The surgeon checks the vascularization of the proposed proximal end using endocyanin green. The stapler port is extended to eight centimeters for specimen extraction and excision. A stapler anvil is placed, and the extraction site is closed. The robotic arms are repositioned to the pelvis, and an anastomosis is performed using a powered EEA stapler.
An air leak test is performed, showing no evidence of a leak. Indocyanin green is used to assess bowel perfusion and the blood supply of the anastomosis.
On a two-week post-operative visit, he was tolerating his diet and awaiting oncology evaluation for chemotherapy.
